I have been self employed most of my professional life and ran a high stress lifestyle. I have had 2 divorces which was also impactful on my mental health.   I started to search for answers a few years ago as I noticed my ability to make decisions had deteriorated, possibly from stress. I began to develop tremors in my right arm mid this year and am now being treated for PD. Due to medication my tremors are lessoning but still present.   I am having some side effects from my medications, mostly constipation and ED which is now subsided but I am taking Testosterone.   I can record my mental alertness levels and change of ability to accomplish tasks if it is helpful. I do notice I am very slow to get things done and my ability to carry on an in-depth conversation is greatly reduced. I fluctuate in my ability to have a lively conversation.   I sense that my will power to resist certain things has been reduced. I normally can easily avoid alcohol when I feel it is not a good time to drink but lately if others are around I have not been able to resist the temptation.  I do not have a drinking issue but I wanted to avoid drinks as I am lined up for surgery on my knee on Oct 27th.  Other things to know about me;  - I have hearing aids and hearing issues at times.  - I am having my left knee replaced Oct 27th through a private clinic.   - I have ADHD which I have been able to work with over my life.  - If you want more info on my journey with PD, please ask.

column7: Motivée pour participer aux projets de recherche à Québec et à Montréal. A des périodes off très importants et utilise deux cannes pour marcher. Ne connaît pas si les membres de son famille du côté de son père fait le maladie de Parkinson, mais personne de son côté maternel fait le maladie de Parkinson. Fait des migraines importantes, et a fait des pertes de visions à cause des migraines. Était avec Dre Mélanie Langlois, mais elle est en train de changer de neurologue pour Dr Ronald Postuma.

Did recreational drugs at university then stopped. Tried Sante Cannabis in an attempt to improve sleep and to address pain.    Took:	CBD 25:1  		CBD 10:13  		Nabilone 0.5 mg  Didn't notice any improvement, although sleep was better with the nabilone.  Gets back aches from bad posture.  Short term memory is not as bad as my long term (part of the issue might be lack of attention)  Lost sense of smell 5 to 7 years before diagnosis.  Played hockey as a kid (1 concussion), and again as an adult for about 15 years (checked into the boards once lost consciousness for a few seconds) did a lot of skiing when younger. At University played varsity ice hockey, basketball, volleyball and tennis. Played competitive squash and taught tennis and squash.
She is not sure how long she has been drinking coffee, but she has been slowly reducing how much of it she drinks daily.

MRI head with gad 01-2018: increased T2 signal in the right periventricular and R insula, increased compared to previous, small area of new focal enhancement.  Father action tremor in his 40s. Paternal uncle PD in his 60s. Identical twin brother also developped Bell's palsy in 2014.

I do exercise, especially walking, but not regularly.  Alcohol still consumed very, very occasionally (e.g. birthday).  Regular alcohol and cigarette amounts in the past varied - it was a long time ago ,difficult to assign amount.

column7: As a former high school art teacher in poorly ventilated rooms, I was exposed to heavy metals (from glazes of copper enamel & ceramic kilns which were in classrooms). We were not trained to use masks or gloves when cleaning surfaces. I had lead, barium, cadmium & silver found in my hair analysis when I was still teaching.

I fall asleep readily, but wake regularly during the night to urinate. I had cancer of the prostate and lymph nodes, and was treated with radiation and female hormones from 2017 to 2022, but have been declared as being in remission. I do not know my uncles/aunts and cousins because my father's career caused my family to move regularly across Canada. I have a brother who is an alcoholic drug user, but don't know whether or not he has Parkinsons.  I stopped drinking upon diagnosis with Parkinsons in February 2023.

column7: Father had Parkinson's, Lewy Body, and level 4 Alzheimer's as determined by a brain section at death.

Both my parents also had polyneuropathies.  Two of my 4 siblings have neuropathy in both their feet.   I think it is caused by our environment.  Our family were residents of NIgeria for many years.  I can elaborate if you like.  905-682-3230

Wondered if dad had undiagnosed Lewy Body Disease. Presented with slow movements, shuffling gate, hallucinations, psychiatric symptoms, paranoid thinking, etc. Never diagnosed while he was in long-term care.

column7: My mom and her two sisters had dementia/alzheimer's disease.  My brother has epilepsy.
